objective: Consolidate AMR into one bounded lower-tier attempt, objective proof, and surgical planned-tier correction loop
prefetch: rg AMR/DDR/tier ownership across skills, router, validators, README, and architecture docs
read selector, selector tests, select CLI, context packet, and manifest contract
constraints: Planner records difficulty and proof, not DDR or model names
Lower-tier attempts require bounded caller-supplied eligibility and objective proof
Planned-tier correction preserves accepted hunks and receives exact failing evidence
Only independently accepted hunks may be protected; otherwise surgical pilot eligibility fails
Ordinary proof and trust policy remain authoritative
acceptance_criteria: One AMR attempt/proof/correction loop replaces DDR duplication
Runtime and CLI prove explicit lower-tier attempt with planned-tier correction metadata
A typed correction packet and deterministic scope check reject unproved preservation or worker-supplied authority
Skills are concise for beginners and preserve advanced overrides
Focused Go, skill-eval, core, and release checks are executed
model_tier_reason: Cross-runtime routing semantics, validator migration, skill consolidation, and compatibility risk
escalation_triggers: Existing manifest compatibility requires a user-visible migration choice
A change would weaken trust, proof, or delivery authorization
Required sync target contains newer useful drift
Scoped tests reveal an unrelated blocking failure
